assumptions: No prior Intercom account configuration or successful use exists.
The developer has the browser and command-line tools listed by the selected path.

developer_goal: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.

first_success.outcome: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.
first_success.observable_signal: The REST API returns a successful response containing the new contact ID.

route_selection.surface: Developer Hub app + Access Token + POST /contacts
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official hosted quickstart and cURL when documented; stop at the first observable successful happy-path result.
route_selection.selected: Developer Hub app + Access Token + POST /contacts

candidates.name: Developer Hub app + Access Token + POST /contacts
candidates.reason: It is the specified official path to the first-success boundary.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to Intercom and open Developer Hub for a workspace.
Create an app and copy its access token.
required_path.observable_result: An app access token is available.

required_path.action: POST the documented contact payload to /contacts with the access token and required headers.
required_path.observable_result: Intercom returns the created contact object.

required_path.action: Confirm the response contains a contact ID and the submitted identifying data.
required_path.observable_result: A new contact ID is visible in the successful response.

prerequisites.description: An Intercom workspace and Developer Hub access.
A terminal with cURL or another HTTPS client.

external_gates.description: The user must have permission to create an app in the target Intercom workspace.
The access token must be authorized for the contact operation.

platform_outcomes.description: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.

excluded.reason: This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.

sources.title: Getting started with an integration
evidence_supported: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.
The REST API returns a successful response containing the new contact ID.

sources.title: Create a contact
evidence_supported: An authenticated POST /contacts request creates an Intercom contact and returns the contact object.

uncertainties.question: Does fresh-account UI wording differ from the documented Create a contact path?
uncertainties.impact: A human should judge minor navigation differences without moving the first-success boundary.
